title: Breadth-First Search
code:
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
import qualified Data.Set as Set
import qualified Data.Sequence as Seq

type Graph a = Map.Map a [a]

bfs :: Ord a => Graph a -> a -> [a]
bfs graph start = go (Seq.singleton start) Set.empty []
  where
    go Seq.Empty _ acc = reverse acc
    go (node Seq.:<| rest) visited acc
      | Set.member node visited = go rest visited acc
      | otherwise =
          let visited' = Set.insert node visited
              neighbors = filter (`Set.notMember` visited') (Map.findWithDefault [] node graph)
          in go (rest Seq.>< Seq.fromList neighbors) visited' (node : acc)
description: BFS using a `Data.Sequence` as a FIFO queue and a `Set` for visited tracking. The graph is represented as an adjacency map. Demonstrates Haskell's persistent data structures for queue operations.
